dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.selectClusterView: Please select a cluster to view monitoring data

alerts.title: Cluster Alerts
alerts.overcommitted: Cluster resources are overcommitted (limits exceed capacity)

clusters.title: Clusters
clusters.kubeconfigPathPlaceholder: Please enter the full path to the kubeconfig file
clusters.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete cluster {name}?
clusters.prometheusUrlHint: Optional Prometheus base URL for metrics proxy, e.g. http://prometheus:9090

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete node {nodeName}?
nodes.deleteWarning: This action will remove the node from the cluster. If there are still pods running on the node, you can force delete.
nodes.deleteForceEvict: Evict all pods from the node
nodes.deleteForceRemove: Remove the node from the cluster

nodePool.title: Node Pools
nodePool.discoveredHint: Cloud node pools only expose an ID; set a display name via Configure
nodePool.discoveredInfoDesc: Cloud providers such as ACK only sync the node pool ID (e.g. np32a...) into Kubernetes, not the console display name. Use Configure to set a local display name, labels, and autoscaling policy. These settings are stored in the cluster and do not modify the cloud node pool.
nodePool.discoveredDeleteHint: Cloud node pools are managed by the cloud platform and cannot be deleted here
nodePool.displayNameHint: Enter the node pool name from the ACK console for easier identification
nodePool.confirmDeleteOverlay: Delete local configuration only? The cloud node pool will not be removed.
nodePool.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this node pool?

taintEffects.PreferNoSchedule: PreferNoSchedule (tries to avoid new pods scheduling)
taintEffects.NoExecute: NoExecute (prevents new pods and evicts existing pods)

addNodeModal.title: Add Node
addNodeModal.pleaseEnterSSHKeyFile: Please enter SSH key file path

pods.title: Pods
pods.name: Pod Name
pods.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this Pod?
pods.noPodsFound: No pods found in this namespace

restartPolicy.title: Restart Policy
restartPolicy.recreateSuccess: Pod recreated successfully with new restart policy
restartPolicy.createSuccess: New Pod created successfully with the specified restart policy
restartPolicy.confirm: Are you sure you want to update the Pod's restart policy?
restartPolicy.description: Restart policy determines the restart behavior of containers in the Pod
restartPolicy.alertDescription: Only standalone Pods (not managed by controllers like Deployment, StatefulSet, etc.) can have their restart policy modified. For controller-managed Pods, configure the restart policy at the controller level.
restartPolicy.warningDesc: Only standalone Pods (not managed by controllers like Deployment, StatefulSet, etc.) can have their restart policy modified. For controller-managed Pods, configure the restart policy at the controller level.
restartPolicy.noChanges: No changes detected in restart policy

podLifecycle.title: Lifecycle Management
podLifecycle.gracePeriodHelp: Time to wait before force killing the pod (seconds)
podLifecycle.waitTimeoutHelp: Maximum time to wait for operation completion (seconds)
podLifecycle.startConfirm: Are you sure you want to start this Pod?
podLifecycle.stopConfirm: Are you sure you want to stop this Pod? The Pod will be deleted.
podLifecycle.restartConfirm: Are you sure you want to restart this Pod? The Pod will be deleted and recreated.
podLifecycle.pauseConfirm: Are you sure you want to pause this Pod?
podLifecycle.resumeConfirm: Are you sure you want to resume this Pod?
podLifecycle.failedPodTip: Pod is in failed state. You can try restarting or check logs for details.
podLifecycle.fetchStatusFailed: Failed to fetch Pod lifecycle status
podLifecycle.fetchHistoryFailed: Failed to fetch Pod lifecycle history

logs.title: Container Logs
logs.fetchFailedRetry: Failed to fetch logs, please try again
logs.connectingStream: Connecting to real-time log stream, please wait...
logs.startStreamFailedNetwork: Failed to start log stream, please check network connection or refresh the page
logs.startStreamFailedDetail:
Failed to start log stream, please check network connection or refresh the page
Error details: {error}

deployments.title: Deployments
deployments.name: Deployment Name
deployments.restartConfirm: Are you sure you want to restart this Deployment?

hpa.title: Horizontal Pod Autoscalers
hpa.noHpa: No HPA configured for this deployment

monitoring.noData: No monitoring data. Ensure metrics-server is installed and pods are running.
monitoring.fetchFailed: Failed to load monitoring data. Rebuild and restart the backend if you recently upgraded.

form.name: Deployment Name
form.namePattern: Name must consist of lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and cannot start or end with a hyphen

probes.commandPlaceholder: Enter commands to execute, one per line

advanced.serviceAccountPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the default service account

nodeAffinity.title: Node Affinity
nodeAffinity.description: Node affinity controls which nodes the Pod can be scheduled on
nodeAffinity.requiredRulesDetail: Pod can only be scheduled to nodes that satisfy at least one rule group
nodeAffinity.preferredRulesDetail: Scheduler will try to place Pod on nodes that satisfy these rules, but will still place it elsewhere if not possible
nodeAffinity.requiredRulesDescription: Pod must be scheduled to nodes that satisfy at least one rule group
nodeAffinity.preferredRulesDescription: Pod will preferentially be scheduled to nodes that satisfy these rules, with each rule having a weight value
nodeAffinity.fieldMatchDescription: Field matching is an advanced feature, typically used to match special node fields such as metadata.
nodeAffinity.valuesPlaceholder: Enter label values and press Enter, multiple values allowed

affinity.detail: Schedule Pods onto the same topology domain (node, zone, etc.) as matching Pods. Useful for co-location.
affinity.pleaseEnterTopologyKey: Please select or enter topology key
affinity.namespacesHint: Leave empty for current namespace; specify multiple for cross-namespace matching

antiAffinity.detail: Avoid scheduling Pods onto topology domains that already have matching Pods. Common for HA spread across nodes/zones.
antiAffinity.topologyKeyHint: Typically kubernetes.io/hostname to spread replicas across nodes
antiAffinity.pleaseEnterTopologyKey: Please select or enter topology key

tolerations.title: Tolerations
tolerations.detail: Allow Pods to be scheduled onto nodes with matching taints. Typically paired with node pool taints for dedicated worker nodes.

rollback.title: Rollback
rollback.confirmContent: Are you sure you want to rollback to revision #{revision}?

services.title: Services
services.name: Service Name
services.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this service?

form.name: Service Name
form.namePattern: Name must consist of lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and cannot start or end with a hyphen
form.atLeastOnePort: At least one port must be configured

statefulsets.title: StatefulSets
statefulsets.name: StatefulSet Name
statefulsets.restartConfirm: Are you sure you want to restart this StatefulSet?
statefulsets.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this StatefulSet?
statefulsets.confirmRestart: Are you sure you want to restart this StatefulSet?
statefulsets.rollingUpdateDesc: Pods are updated one at a time in ordinal order
statefulsets.storageClassNamePlaceholder: Leave empty to use default storage class
statefulsets.replicasMustBePositive: Replicas must be a non-negative number

operations.drainConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to drain node {nodeName}?
operations.drainEvictPods: Safely evict all pods from the node
operations.drainOptions: The following options will be applied:
operations.drainOptionLocalData: Do not delete pods with local storage

nodepool.nodepoolNameEmpty: Node pool name cannot be empty
nodepool.fetchFailed: Failed to fetch node pool information

list.failed: Failed to retrieve node pool list

effects.preferNoSchedule: PreferNoSchedule (tries to avoid scheduling new pods)
effects.noExecute: NoExecute (prevents new pods and evicts existing ones)

podTerminal.title: Terminal:{podName}{containerName}
podTerminal.possibleReasons: Possible reasons: container is not ready or pod is not running
podTerminal.podNotExist: Pod '{pod}' does not exist in namespace '{namespace}'
podTerminal.connectionTimeoutError: Connection timed out. The container might not be ready.
podTerminal.noContainersFound: No containers found in this pod

events.title: {resourceKind} Events
events.fetchErrorRetry: Failed to fetch {resourceKind} events, please try again

secrets.dataHint: One key=value pair per line (plain text, sent as stringData)

resourceQuotas.hardHint: One resource=limit per line, e.g. pods=10

prometheus.title: Prometheus Metrics
prometheus.queryFailed: Prometheus query failed. Ensure the cluster has a Prometheus URL configured.
prometheus.noData: No Prometheus data. Verify cAdvisor/kubelet metrics and PromQL match your setup.

trafficTopology.title: Service Traffic Topology
trafficTopology.hint: Paths and NetworkPolicy come from cluster APIs. Terway clusters auto-connect to in-cluster hubble-metrics or Prometheus — no extra setup required.

messages.terway_detected: Terway network detected. Topology is built from cluster APIs.
messages.terway_observable: Auto-connected to in-cluster network observability (Hubble Metrics).
messages.terway_hubble_ready: Terway + Hubble ready. Showing declared topology and L4 metrics.
messages.hubble_available: Hubble components found in cluster; attempting to collect metrics.
messages.cni_unknown: CNI not identified; service topology still available from K8s API.

messages.hubble_no_data: No L4 metrics yet (no traffic or metrics not reported).

labelLogs.hint: Enter a Kubernetes label selector (e.g. app=nginx) to fetch logs from all matching pods.
labelLogs.empty: Enter a label selector and click Fetch Logs
